op.name: random.default_rng
op.notes: Construct a new Generator with default BitGenerator.
op.summary: Construct a new Generator with the default BitGenerator (PCG64).

blocks.text: Construct a new Generator with the default BitGenerator (PCG64).

items.name: seed
items.type: {None, int, array_like[ints], SeedSequence, BitGenerator, Generator, RandomState}, optional

desc_blocks.text:
A seed to initialize the `BitGenerator`. If None, then fresh,
unpredictable entropy will be pulled from the OS. If an ``int`` or
``array_like[ints]`` is passed, then all values must be non-negative and will be
passed to `SeedSequence` to derive the initial `BitGenerator` state. One may also
pass in a `SeedSequence` instance.
Additionally, when passed a `BitGenerator`, it will be wrapped by
`Generator`. If passed a `Generator`, it will be returned unaltered.
When passed a legacy `RandomState` instance it will be coerced to a `Generator`.

blocks.text:
If ``seed`` is not a `BitGenerator` or a `Generator`, a new `BitGenerator`
is instantiated. This function does not manage a default global instance.
See :ref:`seeding_and_entropy` for more information about seeding.

`default_rng` is the recommended constructor for the random number class
`Generator`. Here are several ways we can construct a random
number generator using `default_rng` and the `Generator` class.
Here we use `default_rng` to generate a random float:

blocks.code:
>>> import numpy as np
>>> rng = np.random.default_rng(12345)
>>> print(rng)
Generator(PCG64)
>>> rfloat = rng.random()
>>> rfloat
0.22733602246716966
>>> type(rfloat)
<class 'float'>

blocks.text:
Here we use `default_rng` to generate 3 random integers between 0
(inclusive) and 10 (exclusive):

blocks.code:
>>> import numpy as np
>>> rng = np.random.default_rng(12345)
>>> rints = rng.integers(low=0, high=10, size=3)
>>> rints
array([6, 2, 7])
>>> type(rints[0])
<class 'numpy.int64'>

blocks.text: Here we specify a seed so that we have reproducible results:

If we exit and restart our Python interpreter, we'll see that we
generate the same random numbers again:
